div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China (Johnson)
div.p: 135. Your 225, April 30, noon. The Department approves your instructing various offices as proposed. The Department suggests that, unless you perceive objection, you instruct CaldwellJohn K. Caldwell, Consul General at Tientsin. to assume primary charge, under your direction, of coordinating the American study of this subject and to keep in touch with SansomSir George B. Sansom, Commercial Counselor of the British Embassy in Japan. and Hall-Patch while they are in north China. If you concur in the procedure outlined above, please issue appropriate instructions and repeat the foregoing to Tokyo.
